title: browser.close() is called unconditionally in the finally-less structure, but if waitForSelector throws a non-timeout error the catch block may rethrow before browser.close() executes.
description: The `try` block awaits two `page.waitForSelector` calls and several `page.evaluate` / `page.screenshot` calls. The `catch (err)` block handles the timeout case and does DOM inspection but does NOT rethrow. After the try/catch, `await browser.close()` runs unconditionally. This is actually correct for the timeout case. However, if `page.screenshot`, `page.evaluate`, or `page.locator().getAttribute()` inside the catch block throw (e.g., if the browser process dies), that secondary exception would propagate up and `browser.close()` would be skipped, leaving a dangling Chrome process. A `try/finally` wrapping `browser.close()` would be more robust.

title: Variable `err` inside the catch block is shadowed by a new `const err` from getAttribute, hiding the original caught error.
description: The outer `catch (err)` binds the caught exception to `err`. Inside the catch body, `const err = await page.locator('.thumb').first().getAttribute('data-error')` re-declares `err` as a `const` in the same block scope. In modern JS, `catch (err)` creates a binding scoped to the catch block, so the inner `const err` is a redeclaration in the same scope — this is actually a runtime `SyntaxError` in strict mode or at minimum a redeclaration error in TypeScript. The original exception is completely inaccessible after the inner `const err` is declared.

title: Top-level await on WASM init runs before the ORF read; if init throws, the error message gives no context about which step failed.
description: All five scripts use top-level await. This is intentional and correct for ESM scripts under Bun/Deno. The ordering in `analyze.ts` is: init WASM → read ORF → process → load reference → report. If `init` throws (e.g., wasm file missing), the error is undecorated. This is a very minor dev-UX issue rather than a correctness bug, but worth noting since the wasm path is constructed from `import.meta.url` which can be surprising if the script is run from a different working directory.
